title: Presentation Interface
subtitle: Create Amazing Slides!
overview: This session introduces students to the Presentation Interface, helping them understand menus, toolbars, slides, animations, themes, and presentation tools used to create engaging presentations.

images._note: Add screenshots of presentation software, toolbar sections, themes, student activities, and presentation practice photos.

activity.title: Create Your First Presentation
steps: Present in front of the class

quiz.q: What is the main purpose of a presentation?
options: To organize and share information visually
quiz.explanation: Presentations help us organize and present information visually using slides.

quiz.q: Which software is commonly used to create presentations?
quiz.explanation: Microsoft PowerPoint is one of the most popular presentation software tools.

quiz.q: What is the area called where slide thumbnails are displayed?
quiz.explanation: The Slides Panel displays all slide thumbnails for navigation.
The Ribbon contains tabs, tools, and commands used to create presentations.

quiz.q: Which option is used to add pictures to a slide?
quiz.explanation: The Insert tab is used to add images, shapes, charts, and more.
Transitions are effects shown when moving from one slide to another.

quiz.q: What do Animations do in presentations?
quiz.explanation: Animations add movement effects to text, images, and objects.

quiz.q: Which key starts the Slide Show in PowerPoint?
quiz.explanation: Pressing F5 starts the presentation slideshow from the beginning.

quiz.q: What is a Theme in presentation software?
quiz.explanation: Themes provide ready-made designs, colors, and fonts for presentations.

quiz.q: Which tab is used to change slide design?
quiz.explanation: The Design tab is used to apply themes and customize slide appearance.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of the Title Bar?
quiz.explanation: The Title Bar displays the name of the current presentation file.

quiz.q: What should be added to make slides more attractive?
quiz.explanation: Images and visuals make presentations more engaging and easy to understand.

quiz.q: Which mode is used to present slides to an audience?
quiz.explanation: Slide Show Mode displays slides full-screen for presenting.

quiz.q: What is the best practice for presentation slides?
quiz.explanation: Good presentations use short points, visuals, and readable fonts.

options: To improve confidence and communication skills
quiz.explanation: Practicing presentations helps students improve confidence and public speaking skills.
